entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept and scope of Geography (branches, importance and relationship with other disciplines)
teaching_learning_activities:
Brainstorming:
In manageable groups students brainstorm on the meaning, branches and importance of Geography, and relationship between Geography and other disciplines

Field Observation:
Guide students to observe landforms, vegetations and human activities to support the concept of geography and summarise their importance in relationship to other disciplines
entries.assessment: Basic concepts of Geography are clearly explained
resources: Relevant books, charts, and ICT simulation tools on the concept of Geography
entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems

teaching_learning_activities:

Guide students to observe landforms, vegetations and human activities to support the concept of geography and summarise their importance in relationship to other disciplines
entries.assessment: Basic concepts of Geography are clearly explained
resources: Relevant books, charts, and ICT simulation tools on the concept of Geography
entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept and scope of Geography (branches, importance and relationship with other disciplines)
teaching_learning_activities:
Brainstorming:
In manageable groups students brainstorm on the meaning, branches and importance of Geography, and relationship between Geography and other disciplines

entries.assessment: Basic concepts of Geography are clearly explained

entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the Earth (origin, shape, size, position and movements of the earth in the solar system, and parallels and meridians)
teaching_learning_activities:
Brainstorming: 
Guide students to brainstorm and present about the origin, shape, size, position and movements of the earth in the solar system, and parallels and meridians

Field Observation:
Guide students to observe the Earth’s surface and identify some of the components of the solar system

Project work:
Group students to design a model on the components of the solar system (size, shape, distance, position), and then present in class
entries.assessment: The Earth is described correctly
resources: Relevant books, globe, picture of solar system, relevant ICT simulation tools and models on the planet earth
entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the Earth (origin, shape, size, position and movements of the earth in the solar system, and parallels and meridians)
teaching_learning_activities:
Brainstorming: 
Guide students to brainstorm and present about the origin, shape, size, position and movements of the earth in the solar system, and parallels and meridians

Field Observation:
Guide students to observe the Earth’s surface and identify some of the components of the solar system

Project work:
Group students to design a model on the components of the solar system (size, shape, distance, position), and then present in class
entries.assessment: The Earth is described correctly
resources: Relevant books, globe, picture of solar system, relevant ICT simulation tools and models on the planet earth
entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
entries.specific_competence: 1.2   Demonstrate an understanding of the major features of the Earth surface
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe major features of the Earth’s surface (continents, oceans, major relief features, large inland water bodies and vegetation zones)
teaching_learning_activities:
Questions &Answers:
Guide students to mention landforms available in their surroundings

Field observation:
Guide students to visit the nearby landforms, vegetation and water bodies available in their communities to examine the formation, distribution and their importance to the communities

Group Discussion:
Guide students to discuss the size and distribution of continents, then present in the class
entries.assessment: The major features of the Earth are correctly described
resources: Environment, relevant books, relief maps, globe/models, and relevant ICT simulation tools
entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe major features of the Earth’s surface (continents, oceans, major relief features, large inland water bodies and vegetation zones)
teaching_learning_activities:
Questions &Answers:
Guide students to mention landforms available in their surroundings

Field observation:
Guide students to visit the nearby landforms, vegetation and water bodies available in their communities to examine the formation, distribution and their importance to the communities

Group Discussion:
Guide students to discuss the size and distribution of continents, then present in the class
entries.assessment: The major features of the Earth are correctly described
resources: Environment, relevant books, relief maps, globe/models, and relevant ICT simulation tools
entries.references: Geography Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)
